item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a tea aficionado (oolong, especially), I really like this one. Especially since there are missing pieces to the set. I could really see this as an MC&D item, though. Either as an auction item, or for use by the bartender at one of the clubhouses.

Aaaand now I've got my brain turning out an outline for a small tale about this….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I normally content myself to lurking and upvoting, but i felt the need to comment here. I was originally not going to vote, since I felt it was uninteresting. However, as some of the other commenters have pointed out, there is no maximum volume listed to the spoon's effect. So, in theory, someone could drop the spoons into the ocean, and just fuck everything up. Also, add the fact that there are three spoons not in foundation custody, and that fridge logic/horror made me upvote.
I do feel that the article could use a rewrite to up the fridge horror, but as for me, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too many numbers, too much irrelevant detail. It could be way simpler and still retain its charm, because it has some charm, at least: Magical tea spoons are charming

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ms. █████ stated that SCP-1067 was purely an impulse-buy, and without precedence. Subject was administered a class-C amnesiac before being released.

Er… is this really a situation that requires administering "amnesiacs"? What, is she going to find out about the foundation's existence because the teaspoons she bought for her brother mysteriously disappeared? Hell, it would be simpler to just replace them with mundane copies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Initially this struck me as a kind of mediocre SCP that I wouldn't mind owning, but it occurred to me that there are no maximum volumes listed in the descriptions. Unless I'm missing something, dropping either -b or -c into the Atlantic ocean would either instantly or over a period of time transform all the Earth's seas into tea. I'm not sure what kind of End of World scenario Tea World would be, but I think that the Foundation might want to put some priority on locating and recovering -e, -f, and -g just in case…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be great for neutralizing SCP-505.
yeah, this is an obligatory comment for all SCPs that turn liquids into other liquids

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While it certainly has potential, I kept waiting for the shoe to drop, and it really never did. These are certainly interesting items, but IMO, hardly dangerous or anomalous enough to warrant and SCP rating. I would love for this article to be expanded upon, though. I think there's certainly a good amount of potential here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still really do not see the appeal of this. It's a set of spoons with some tangentially tea-related properties, with some irritating stylistic/technical choices that I really can't get behind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have to say I quite like this one, and the prospect of someone turning all of the Earth's oceans and seas into milk or tea is somewhat horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea a lot, upvoted.

Also, I can see the second spoon… *Sunglasses* really causing a stir.

YEEEEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Marvellous. I like this one.

As a compulsive tea-drinker myself, the concept of this SCP basically being a magical tea-set is strangely believable. I can imagine it being the entirely ordinary possession of an extremely abnormal being (with excellent taste - Earl Grey is awful with sugar); somewhat similar to 'giant's crockery', the enormous versions of ordinary plates, cups and cutlery of which fairy tales are so fond. Given this (albeit idiosyncratic) interpretation, I would appreciate some sort of personal marking, such as etched or engraved initials, being present on SCP-1067-a. But that's just me.

Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as the cut goes, I don't think so… But if you somehow were able to induce someone to SWALLOW one, it would most certainly turn the liquid contents of their stomach into tea/milk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blackboxing in the containment procedures is distracting - you could easily remove some of the fine detail of the containment location to avoid this.

The object itself is well described, if a little bit lightweight. Not a bad attempt, but this is a no-vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) If spoon-a was applied to open cut would the blood inside the person's body turn into tea?

2) If a liquid is simultaneously stirred by two different spoons does it turn into a mixture of the liquids each produces?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, but the Foundation would probably just file it in the Log Of Anomalous Items and call it a day. My issue with this is that there's absolutely no reason to experiment with it or do anything besides put it on a shelf and forget about it forever. The only real hook here is that there are probably other spoons out there, but the focus of the article is on the ones that the Foundation has in its possession. If you focus on that unknown element, this could be a lot stronger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that we have magic spoons that turn liquids into milk and tea, all that's missing from out tea party is… THE PHILOSOPHER'S SCONE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In response to the repeated criticism that this SCP is not very compelling, I can only say that out of my current ideas I chose a less ambitious one to start with. I will continue to work on this article periodically, as well as some of my other ideas. Whether they will be more successful than this one is yet to be seen however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You did not miss anything that nobody else did. Initially I wrote this with six spoons, all of which had abilities that could be used in the creation of tea, including heating. However my first version proved to be unwieldy and convoluted, so I scaled it down a bit into something more manageable. I may well put some of the original material back in the fullness of time. As for the stylistic issues, bear in mind that this is my first ever SCP, so I hope to produce better and more interesting work in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs some serious stylistic help. It's also not particularly interesting, though I will admit finding it mildly amusing. Shame that it seems to make cold tea, unless I missed something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. G. █████

Dr. Ears? I love that guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will rewrite this article in the future naturally, I originally wrote about six spoons (the entire set), but cut it down because the article became disjointed and messy. I plan to re-introduce the rest of the set in the future, providing that I don't get a massive negative response for the article over the next few days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry if I bored you, though a "spoon that alters things it is used to mix" would certainly hold any scientist's attention in the real world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could use a rewrite, but is good enough to not downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just feels really boring; "spoon that alters things it is used to mix" isn't really a very compelling idea; without any clear drawbacks, an object that just turns certain things into certain other specific things isn't really very neat.

I imagine something could be done to make this more interesting, but I'm not sure what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bleh. This just bores me, and the writing is subpar. I recommend referring to someone who's good at helping people with this kind of thing (read: not me).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tea spoons. I love it, especially because the case can hold more of these.

There's still a couple typos and whatnot. "Locker" shouldn't be capitalized and there's a missing break between paragraphs. Also, "SCP-1067-b has also demonstrated a second, equally enigmatic ability." kind of breaks tone. Just say that it has a second anomalous property when describing it.

EDIT: Oh. And does the sucrose effect have to be with sucrose in water, or sucrose in any liquid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "A small label on the bottom of SCP-1067 reads 'Property of J.L.P., Stardate 3467.1'".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mass spectrometry of samples from SCP-1067-a, b and c confirm the composition of these items to be primarily brass with Stirling-silver plating.

a: Sterling silver, not Stirling-silver.

b: i'm no expert, but it's my understanding that sterling silver isn't used to plate things - fine silver is. sterling silver is for making whole pieces.

c: brass seems an odd base material for plating; copper would be more likely, i think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it could be streamlined.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Accidental double post. Mobile phones are stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the case doesn't have any anomalies itself, should it really get a designation? I mean, it's important to note, especially the slots for missing spoons, but it doesn't feel right to me for it to be -a.

Second nit-pick, for -c "common shop-bought variety" seems a little off tone. I'd suggest just cutting that entire bit in parentheses.

Other than that, I like it. I particularly like how the Earl Gray spoon seems to not approve of sweet tea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry you think that. What do you think is missing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha! sorry about that, that was just carelessness on my part

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeh. Not terribly interesting, slightly convoluted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, I sort of like the idea of a container that contains itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1067-a is a hinged leather-bound container 25.50x14.2x2.50cm in dimension. The inside of SCP-1067-a is lined with blue velvet, and appears to be designed to contain SCP-1067-a to SCP-1067-d, however spaces indicate that there are four (4) missing components.

SCP-1067-a appears to be designed to contain SCP-1067-a? This, and a few other inconsistencies of language prevent me from upvoting. As a whole the idea isn't terribly interesting but it's by no means bad enough for me to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1067-c could make a great Hydro-electric energy producer, other than that this doesn't do much for me, +1 for how well it's written.
